text: vv. 14-16: If we assume, with the best manuscripts, that we are to read ‘seven’ in verse 14 and ‘both’ in verse 16, it is difficult to see how the two verses can be reconciled; unless we are to understand that the possessed man defied the power of both names, those of Jesus and Paul. (In a fragment which dates from the second century a.d., we find ‘both’ used for ‘all’.)

Here, as in 18.17, it is not clear whether the Jew mentioned was a Christian or not; but it seems the name must have been one which St Luke’s readers would be expected to recognize without introduction (cf. 17.6). It is not likely that this is the Alexander mentioned in I Tim. 1.20; II Tim. 4.14.
